# TymeUI AI Guide

- Addon entry point in [source/TymeUI/Initialize.lua](source/TymeUI/Initialize.lua) wires ElvUI callbacks (EP:HookInitialize), seeds AceDB saved var `TymeUIDB`, sets log level (DebugMode flag), checks ElvUI minimum version (X-ElvUIVersion in toc), then calls `InitializeModules`.
- Toc metadata in [source/TymeUI/TymeUI.toc](source/TymeUI/TymeUI.toc) declares saved vars, required deps (SimpleAddonManager, ElvUI), version/flavor tags, and load order (Initialize.lua → Libraries → Core → Modules → Brokers).
- Core module loader in [source/TymeUI/Core/Core.lua](source/TymeUI/Core/Core.lua) registers modules, injects loggers, updates cooldown settings, and finally loads chat commands from [source/TymeUI/Core/Commands.lua](source/TymeUI/Core/Commands.lua) (`/addon`, `/addons`, `/rl`, `/edit`, `/opt` aliases open SAM, reload UI, toggle EditMode, or open settings).
- Static popups for reload/reset live in [source/TymeUI/Core/StaticPopups.lua](source/TymeUI/Core/StaticPopups.lua); `TYMEUI:StaticPopup_ResetProfile()` wipes `ProfileAddons` and reloads, and `:StaticPopup_ReloadUI()` prompts a forced reload.
- Constants and media registration sit in [source/TymeUI/Core/Constants.lua](source/TymeUI/Core/Constants.lua) (layout defaults, color tables, sound/font lists) and [source/TymeUI/Core/SharedMedia.lua](source/TymeUI/Core/SharedMedia.lua) (registers sounds into LSM under `Interface\AddOns\TymeUI\Media\`).
- Utility helpers live under [source/TymeUI/Core/Functions](source/TymeUI/Core/Functions): `Log.lua` injects `LogInfo/Warning/Debug/Trace`, `Table.lua` provides `Crush/Join/GetOrCreate` and color helpers, `String.lua` handles color/text parsing, `Core.lua` offers event/throttle helpers, and `Chat()` centralizes colored chat output.
- WoW API wrappers: prefer the modern `C_AddOns.*` and `C_CVar.*` functions when adding code; keep the existing fallback pattern used in Initialize/Commands for compatibility.
- Profiles system: main controller [source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Core.lua](source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Core.lua) is the only module loaded by [source/TymeUI/Modules/Load.xml](source/TymeUI/Modules/Load.xml); it reads per-addon profile states from `TYMEUI.db.profile.ProfileAddons`, aborts if ElvUI install isn’t complete, and registers `/resetprofile`.
- Profile modules live under [source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons](source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons); each creates a `module = TYMEUI:NewModule(...)`, sets `Name/Enabled/ReloadUI`, implements `Initialize()` guarded for idempotence, then registers via `PF:RegisterProfile(module)`. Always honor `PF:CanLoadProfileForAddon()` before mutating addon globals.
- ElvUI profile loader [source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/ElvUI.lua](source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/ElvUI.lua) merges large config fragments into `E.db`/`E.private` with `F.Table.Crush`, disables many frames, and adjusts movers/datatexts; it assumes ElvUI tables already exist.
- EditModeExpanded profile [source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/EditModeExpanded.lua](source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/EditModeExpanded.lua) seeds `EditModeExpandedADB` with a custom layout keyed by current layout name; ensure layout table shapes match addon expectations before changing.
- Details profile [source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/Details.lua](source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/Details.lua) imports an encoded profile string into `_detalhes`; avoid editing the string unless regenerating via Details export.
- SimpleAddonManager profile [source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/SimpleAddonManager.lua](source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/SimpleAddonManager.lua) builds a “Default-Addons” set enabling a long list of addons and drives SAM via a scripted chat command; update the `addonsToEnable` table to change the set.
- AuctionHouse module [source/TymeUI/Modules/AuctionHouse/Core.lua](source/TymeUI/Modules/AuctionHouse/Core.lua) plays a sound on auction sold events but is commented out in [source/TymeUI/Modules/Load.xml](source/TymeUI/Modules/Load.xml); include it there to enable.
- Broker stubs exist under [source/TymeUI/Brokers](source/TymeUI/Brokers) (Load.xml + data broker modules) but are not currently referenced by the module loader.
- Saved variable usage: AceDB profile defaults are defined in [source/TymeUI/Initialize.lua](source/TymeUI/Initialize.lua) (`profile.ProfileAddons = {}`) and consumed by Profiles; resetting profiles wipes this table and forces reload.
- Logging: enable `TYMEUI.DebugMode` in [source/TymeUI/Initialize.lua](source/TymeUI/Initialize.lua) to raise log verbosity (LogLevel 4); use `F.Log.InjectLogger` to add log methods to new modules.
- Validate Lua syntax after edits: run `get_errors` on touched files to catch missing `end`/mismatched brackets before testing in-game.
- Deployment (Windows): use VS Code task “Deploy TymeUI” or run `build/deploy.cmd "${workspaceFolder}\source\TymeUI" "D:\Games\World of Warcraft\_retail_\Interface\AddOns\TymeUI"` to clean target and xcopy the addon; ensure WoW is closed to avoid lock issues.
- Required dependencies at runtime: ElvUI (matching X-ElvUIVersion), SimpleAddonManager, and any profiled addons referenced by profile modules (ElvUI, Details, EditModeExpanded, etc.). `F.IsAddOnEnabled()` guards profile application; if an addon is missing or disabled, the profile loader aborts and logs.
- Extending: to add a new profiled addon, create a module under Profiles/Addons, set `module.Enabled`, implement `LoadProfile`/`Initialize`, set `module.ReloadUI = true` when changes need a reload, and register it in [source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/Load.xml](source/TymeUI/Modules/Profiles/Addons/Load.xml).
- Extending core modules: register via `TYMEUI:RegisterModule(name)` and include your file in Core/Load.xml or Modules/Load.xml; follow the pre-init/Initialize pattern and call `ModulePreInitialize` for logging.
- Media additions: drop assets under [source/TymeUI/Media](source/TymeUI/Media) and register via `AddMedia` in [source/TymeUI/Core/SharedMedia.lua](source/TymeUI/Core/SharedMedia.lua) so LSM picks them up.
- Safety: many profile writers mutate global addon DB tables directly; prefer `F.Table.Crush` to merge rather than assign wholesale to avoid nil tables and maintain partial compatibility across addon versions.
- Quick test loop: deploy, launch WoW, enable `/console scriptErrors 1`, run `/rl`, and verify chat commands (`/addon`, `/addons`, `/edit`, `/opt`) plus profile application; watch for missing saved-var tables and create with `F.Table.GetOrCreate` before writing.
